tools.name: get_versions
tools.description: Report the webforJ versions this server knows about. Returns the latest stable release, the current in-development SNAPSHOT (from the main-branch pom.xml), and an array of every major with its highest observed tag plus a `status` flag: "development" (the active SNAPSHOT line — actively changing), "s

tools.name: create_project
tools.description: Returns the Maven archetype command that scaffolds a new webforJ project, plus the follow-up commands to change into the generated directory and start the dev server. Relay the Maven command to the user via the host shell tool without modifying its arguments. Set showOptions=true to list available a

tools.name: get_document
tools.description: Return the full content of an indexed webforJ documentation or sample file. Use this as a follow-up to search_knowledge_base when a search result excerpt is not enough and you need the complete page — migration guides, tutorials, long API docs. Pass the documentId from a search result.

tools.name: search_knowledge_base
tools.description: Search webforJ documentation, JavaDoc, and code samples for any class, method, annotation, or component, and for CSS styling concepts and rules (palette semantics, seed vs h/s, :not([theme]) pattern, dark mode, ::part limits, table styling). Returns matching snippets with titles, categories, and rel

tools.name: styles_get_component
tools.description: Return the real CSS styling surface of a DWC web component: CSS custom properties, shadow parts, reflected attributes, and slots. Accepts a DWC tag ("dwc-button") or a webforJ Java class name ("Button", "TextField"). Use mode="list" for every valid tag, mode="map" for Java -> tag mappings. Call this

tools.name: styles_list_tokens
tools.description: Return the authoritative list of global --dwc-* tokens (palette seeds, color shades, surfaces, spacing, typography, borders, etc.). Use this for any "which tokens are available for X" question — DO NOT ask the knowledge base to list tokens; it returns prose and the answer will be hallucinated. Filte

tools.name: styles_validate_tokens
tools.description: Validate every --dwc-* reference in CSS, Java, MDX, or Markdown text against the real DWC global tokens and component CSS variables. Returns the list of invalid tokens with line numbers and ranked similar-name suggestions. Run this on any generated or edited stylesheet before writing it to disk.

tools.name: fqcn_validate
tools.description: Validate fully qualified class names against the catalog of types declared in webforJ source for the target version. Accepts either an explicit list of FQCNs or a Java snippet (the handler extracts its import statements). Returns unknown FQCNs with ranked similar-name suggestions. FQCNs outside the

tools.name: create_theme
tools.description: Generate a webforJ theme from a primary HSL color. Returns two content blocks: a JSON summary with theme metadata, @AppTheme / @StyleSheet snippets, and recommended file paths, plus a second text block prefixed "CSS_CONTENT:" containing the full stylesheet.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_knowledge_base · Private data: get_document, search_knowledge_base, styles_list_tokens, styles_validate_tokens, create_theme · Public sink: styles_list_tokens · Destructive: styles_validate_tokens, fqcn_validate.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_knowledge_base · Private data: get_document, search_knowledge_base, styles_list_tokens, styles_validate_tokens, create_theme · Public sink: styles_list_tokens · Destructive: styles_validate_tokens, fqcn_validate

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
